reasoning:
All three senators independently and unanimously reached the same conclusion: the submission 'test question' lacks the minimum semantic content required for meaningful deliberation. There is no stated question, no context, no stakeholders, no tradeoffs, and no measurable outcome. Deliberating on a null input produces noise, not signal, and any verdict issued would be arbitrary rather than reasoned.

Beyond the immediate case, all senators raised the systemic concern that accepting malformed inputs sets a normalizing precedent. If the Senate will deliberate on anything regardless of quality, it invites low-effort submissions and degrades the signal-to-noise ratio of the deliberation record over time. The integrity of the deliberative system depends on enforcing a minimum quality contract at the point of entry.

If this was an integration or smoke test of the Senate infrastructure, it should be routed through a dedicated test harness rather than the live deliberation pipeline. The system functioned correctly — senators were spawned, deliberated, and converged — confirming the pipeline is operational.
implementation: 1. Reject this submission with a request for resubmission containing: (a) a clear, specific question, (b) relevant context and background, (c) defined success criteria or decision dimensions. 2. Consider adding an input validation layer to the Senate intake pipeline that requires minimum fields before a case enters deliberation. 3. If this was a system test, establish a dedicated test-mode flag or test case ID prefix (e.g., 'senate-TEST-*') to prevent test submissions from polluting the live deliberation record.
dissent: There is no dissent. All three senators — Pragmatist, Purist, and Skeptic — independently and unanimously voted to reject. No minority position exists in this deliberation.
